mnemonics: GROUP BY Helps Organize Raw Data Perfectly By Yielding summaries
keyPoints: The GROUP BY clause is used to arrange identical data into groups.
It is often used with aggregate functions (COUNT, SUM, AVG, MAX, MIN) to perform a calculation on each group.
The HAVING clause is used to filter groups based on a condition, unlike WHERE which filters individual rows.
commonMistakes: Using a column in the SELECT list that is not in the GROUP BY clause and not an aggregate function.
Using the WHERE clause to filter groups instead of the HAVING clause.
Forgetting that NULL values are grouped together into their own group.
